Gerande, Aubert, and Scholastique follow him across the mountains to the ruined castle in the gorge of the Dents-du-Midi (MZ-005-030).

Its authority is invoked from Edinburgh to London: the whaler Scoresby laid his polar observations before the Royal Society of Edinburgh, an affiliate of the same tradition of scientific academies. (ACH-005-045)

Winter seals it as they cross: "during the night the temperature fell below zero; ice was already forming on the surface of the Baikal." (MS-029-017) Its outlet feeds the Angara, the river that carries the story on toward Irkutsk.
The brig makes for it and passes within: "the *Forward* was then at the extremity of Lancaster Strait." (ACH-014-006) At its mouth "the crew caught a glimpse of Burney Cape." (ACH-014-003)

It stands at the western end of a vast traverse — the distance between Moscow and Irkutsk, about to be crossed by Strogoff, was three thousand four hundred miles. (MS-005-001) By the older reckoning it is five thousand two hundred versts from Moscow to Irkutsk. (MS-003-066)
tags: [place, france, capital]

The travellers settle down for the night "with the intention of sleeping till we reach Tachkend," (ASC-013-005) a city toward which the swarming pedlars called Sarthes are "evidently ... going." (ASC-006-011) On arrival "we are at Tachkend, and the time-table shows that we stop here two hours and a half," (ASC-014-003) and afterwards "we left Tachkend at precisely eleven o'clock in the morning." (ASC-014-014) Beyond it "the railway had curved toward the south, so as to reach Khodjend." (ASC-014-029)
region: Patagonia

The South Pole Attained is the great polar set-piece of *[[twenty-thousand-leagues|Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea]]s*, in which [[captain-nemo|Captain Nemo]] carries the [[nautilus|Nautilus]] to the geographic South Pole.
Even at the altar she drives a bargain, meeting the terms with "well, let us say twenty per cent., Miss Bluett" (ASC-022-025).
